calendar.matters:
Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award
Off Calendar Feb-05-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: PETITIONERS BUMBLE, INC., BUMBLE TRADING LLC, AND BUMBLE HOLDING LIMITED's Motion To Confirm Arbitration Award.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Petitioners Bumble Inc.; Bumble Trading LLC; and Bumble Holding Limited seek to confirm the arbitration award entered on October 17, 2024, and seek a final judgment based on the award. A party may seek a court judgment confirming an arbitration or appraisal award by filing and serving a petition at least 10 days, but no more than four years after the award is served. (CCP, 1288 and 1288.4) The court must confirm the award as made, unless it corrects or vacates the award, or dismisses the proceeding. (Id. 1286.) If the award is confirmed, an enforceable judgment is entered with the same force and effect as a judgment in a civil action. (Id., 1287.4.)

Petitioners attach the award as Exhibit E to the Vierra Declaration. No proof of service showing when the award was served is attached, but because Petitioners served the Vierra Declaration on Respondent Conor Gray by overnight delivery and mail on January 7, 2025, and the date of hearing is more than 10 days after the award, the court finds compliance with the procedural requirements recited above. The unopposed motion is granted and the award is confirmed. The award demonstrates that Petitioners are entitled to costs they incurred after the offer to compromise was rejected. (Vierra Dec., Ex. C, at p. 4.)

Petitioners are ordered to prepare a final judgment stating substantially as follows: The motion of Bumble Inc.; Bumble Trading LLC; and Bumble Holding Limited, Petitioners, for an order confirming the arbitration award rendered herein on October 17, 2024, came on regularly for hearing before the undersigned on February 4, 2025, upon notice duly and regularly given. Good cause appearing therefor, IT IS ORDERED that the award of the arbitrator dated October 17, 2024, be confirmed. Petitioners are entitled to costs of suit herein.

Petitioners ask for costs to be awarded with the judgment. The court denies the request without prejudice. Petitioners may seek these costs as set out in Rule of Court 3.1700.

Order submitted. Judgment submitted.

Judge: Christine Van Aken; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/CVA)
